2604.28055.title: PROMISE-AD: Progression-aware Multi-horizon Survival Estimation for Alzheimer's Disease Progression and Dynamic Tracking
2604.28055.paper_summary: Individualized Alzheimer's disease (AD) progression prediction requires models that use irregular visits, account for censoring, avoid diagnostic leakage, and provide calibrated horizon risks. We propose PROgression-aware MultI-horizon Survival Estimation for Alzheimer's Disease (PROMISE-AD), a leakage-safe survival framework for predicting conversion from cognitively normal (CN) to mild cognitive impairment (MCI) and from MCI to AD dementia using ADNI/TADPOLE tabular histories. PROMISE-AD converts pre-index visits into tokens with standardized measurements, missingness masks, longitudinal changes, time-normalized slopes, visit timing, and non-diagnostic categorical attributes. A temporal Transformer fuses global, attention-pooled, and latest-visit representations to estimate a progression score and latent discrete-time mixture hazards. Training combines survival likelihood, horizon-specific focal risk loss, progression ranking, hazard smoothness, and mixture-balance regularization, followed by validation-set isotonic calibration for 1-, 2-, 3-, and 5-year risks. In held-out testing across three seeds, PROMISE-AD achieved an integrated Brier score (IBS) of 0.085 $\pm$ 0.012, C-index of 0.808 $\pm$ 0.015, and mean time-dependent AUC of 0.840 $\pm$ 0.081 for CN-to-MCI conversion, yielding the lowest IBS among compared methods. For MCI-to-AD conversion, PROMISE-AD achieved the highest C-index (0.894 $\pm$ 0.018) and near-ceiling 5-year discrimination (AUROC 0.997 $\pm$ 0.003; AUPRC 0.999 $\pm$ 0.001), although some baselines had lower IBS. Ablations and interpretability supported longitudinal change features, fused temporal representations, mixture hazards, cognitive and functional measures, APOE4 status, and recent conversion-proximal visits. These findings suggest that progression-aware survival modeling can provide interpretable multi-horizon AD conversion risk estimates.
2604.28055.paper_summary_zh: 個別化的阿茲海默症（AD）進展預測需要使用不規則訪問的模型，考慮到審查，避免診斷洩漏，並提供經校準的風險預測。我們提出了針對阿茲海默症的進展感知多水平生存估計（PROMISE-AD），這是一個安全的生存框架，用於預測從認知正常（CN）到輕度認知障礙（MCI）以及從MCI到AD癡呆的轉變，使用ADNI/TADPOLE的表格歷史數據。PROMISE-AD將前指數訪問轉換為具有標準化測量、缺失掩碼、縱向變化、時間標準化斜率、訪問時間和非診斷類別屬性的標記。時間Transformer融合全球、注意力池化和最新訪問表示，以估計進展分數和潛在的離散時間混合風險。訓練結合生存可能性、特定時間範圍的焦點風險損失、進展排名、風險平滑性和混合平衡正則化，隨後進行1年、2年、3年和5年的驗證集等距校準。在三個隨機種子中進行的保留測試中，PROMISE-AD實現了0.085 $\pm$ 0.012的綜合Brier分數（IBS）、0.808 $\pm$ 0.015的C指數，以及0.840 $\pm$ 0.081的平均時間依賴AUC，用於CN到MCI的轉換，並在比較方法中獲得最低的IBS。對於MCI到AD的轉換，PROMISE-AD實現了最高的C指數（0.894 $\pm$ 0.018）和接近上限的5年區分能力（AUROC 0.997 $\pm$ 0.003；AUPRC 0.999 $\pm$ 0.001），儘管一些基準的IBS較低。消融和可解釋性支持了縱向變化特徵、融合的時間表示、混合風險、認知和功能測量、APOE4狀態以及最近的轉換近端訪問。這些發現表明，進展感知的生存建模可以提供可解釋的多水平AD轉換風險估計。
2604.28055.author: Qing Lyu et.al.
2604.28055.authors: Qing Lyu, Jeremy Hudson, Mohammad Kawas, Yuming Jiang, Chenyu You, Christopher T Whitlow
